description:
## Goal
Bring `ir_interp` to parity with JIT/AOT for list primitives. Today every program with a list literal exits 75 ("Deferred") in interp, silently bypassing matrix gates.

## Independent of the rest of the epic
The interp leg runs raw IR (no reducer, no typer, no folds). This ticket has no functional dependency on E1–E4; can land in parallel.

## Gap
`ir_interp` wires `Prim::MakeTuple` and `Prim::TupleField` but is missing:
- `Prim::MakeList`
- `Prim::ListCons`
- `Prim::ListHead`
- `Prim::ListTail`
- `Prim::IsEmptyList`

The error surfaces as `interp .5.2: prim Discriminant(11) not yet supported` (the discriminant is Rust's `std::mem::discriminant`; variant 11 of `Prim` is `MakeList`).

## Runtime support exists
`runtime/src/fz_value.rs` already provides `ListCons` cells (header + head + tail = 32 bytes), `HeapKind::List`, and `Tag::Ptr` empty-list encoding. This is plumbing, not design.

## Done when
- Five prims wired in `ir_interp` against the existing runtime cell layout.
- `fixtures/list_primitives` runs on the interp leg with the same output as JIT/AOT.
- A probe of `[h | t] = []` raises `match_error` in interp identical in shape to JIT/AOT (BEAM-conform).
- All tests pass; the matrix's `interp` leg no longer reports `Deferred` for list-using fixtures.
title: E5: interp parity — list primitives (MakeList, ListCons, ListHead, ListTail, IsEmptyList)
